title: Objects & Organizational Units

headings.text: What Makes Up an AD Object

overview: In Active Directory (AD), objects represent every manageable resource in your network users, computers, groups, printers, file shares, and more. Organizational Units (OUs) act like folders, grouping these objects for streamlined administration and policy application.

study.overview: In Active Directory (AD), objects represent every manageable resource in your network users, computers, groups, printers, file shares, and more. Organizational Units (OUs) act like folders, grouping these objects for streamlined administration and policy application.
study.whySoc: Use this note as an analyst reference: understand the concept, identify the relevant telemetry, pivot through evidence, and document the decision path clearly.
keyConcepts: What Makes Up an AD Object
workflow: Identify the user, host, process, network flow, or identity object involved.
Collect the highest-value logs first and preserve timestamps and assumptions.

importantCommands.code: Get-ADUser -Identity "Username" -Properties LastLogonDate, LockedOut, Modified, PasswordExpired, PasswordLastSet
